channel.title: NPR Topics: Space
channel.description: NPR coverage of space exploration, space shuttle missions, news from NASA, private space exploration, satellite technology, and new discoveries in astronomy and astrophysics.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A hardworking spacecraft in Mars orbit has gone dark
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Shannon Curry, principal investigator on NASA's MAVEN mission, about the spacecraft's decade of observations of Mars.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Shannon Curry, principal investigator on NASA's MAVEN mission, about the spacecraft's decade of observations of Mars.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: NASA names crew for Artemis III mission
item.description: NASA has named the crew of next year's Artemis III mission. The four astronauts will orbit the Earth and practice rendezvous techniques and other tasks to simulate skills needed for a lunar landing.
item.content:encoded: NASA has named the crew of next year's Artemis III mission. The four astronauts will orbit the Earth and practice rendezvous techniques and other tasks to simulate skills needed for a lunar landing.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Meet Astronaut Andre Douglas — ready to bring more moon joy — on Artemis III
item.description: NASA announced the Artemis III crew on Tuesday. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Andre Douglas who will serve as a mission specialist.
item.content:encoded: NASA announced the Artemis III crew on Tuesday. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Andre Douglas who will serve as a mission specialist.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts on the 'highly complex' Artemis III lunar training mission
item.description: The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.
item.content:encoded:
The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion could set back NASA's plans to put astronauts on the moon
item.description: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.content:encoded: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
item.description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.
item.content:encoded:
A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.

(Image credit: John Raoux)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: NASA lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones at the top of the list
item.description: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four U.S. companies.
item.content:encoded:
The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four U.S. companies.

(Image credit: NASA via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Space Nerd Club discusses new findings from the James Webb telescope
item.description: New findings from the James Webb Space Telescope include cloudy weather on an exoplanet, and clues to the origins of one of Neptune's moons.
item.content:encoded: New findings from the James Webb Space Telescope include cloudy weather on an exoplanet, and clues to the origins of one of Neptune's moons.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
item.description: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
item.description: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel
